vision_statement: Decifer is a cloud-hosted, multi-user trading bot platform where friends and family of the founder connect via a web interface, link their own IBKR broker accounts, and configure their own risk profiles — with the core bot having proven profitability on paper before any live capital is risked.
current_stage_assessment: Decifer is in the paper_trading_single_account stage. The core signal pipeline, order routing, risk management, and basic dashboard are built (19k+ lines across 26 modules), but test infrastructure shows 0% pass rate with logged errors across agents, data collection, and orders — meaning the system cannot be trusted as stable. The immediate priority must be stabilising and validating the existing system before adding new signal or architecture features.
executive_summary: Decifer has substantial engineering depth — a 9-dimension signal model, 6-agent consensus pipeline, ML engine, backtester, options flow, multi-account position aggregation, and smart execution — but the project is at a critical inflection point where code volume has outpaced validation. The test suite reports 0% pass rate with errors spanning core modules (agents, orders, data_collector), which means no reliable baseline exists for measuring whether the bot is actually working as intended during paper trading. The feature pipeline is heavily overloaded with 50 active items across multiple stages simultaneously, creating sequencing risk: multi-user cloud architecture features are being designed in parallel with foundational single-account paper trading fixes. The research base is solid and well-cited, but several research-backed features (IC-weighted scoring, fractional Kelly, multi-timeframe confirmation) are backlogged or active without the prerequisite measurement infrastructure (alpha decay tracking, rolling IC, walk-forward validation) being in place first. The project needs a hard reset to: fix tests, validate paper trading behaviour, then sequence features by stage gate.

product_roadmap.goal: Achieve a green test suite, confirm the paper trading loop is executing correctly end-to-end, and establish a measurable performance baseline.
product_roadmap.rationale: Nothing built on top of a broken foundation can be trusted. A 0% test pass rate means we cannot verify that signals, orders, risk limits, or agent consensus are working correctly. All subsequent phases depend on knowing the system behaves as designed. This must be resolved before any new features are added.
features: Fix agent JSON parse errors — agents.py:442 returning non-JSON responses
Fix data_collector network/timeout error handling so tests pass in offline/mock mode
Fix orders.py IBKR connection stub issues causing test failures (reqPnL, openTrades, reqGlobalCancel)
Audit and fix all 32 test files to achieve >90% pass rate
Add end-to-end smoke test: signal → agent consensus → order → risk check → paper fill
Confirm paper trade loop is writing OPEN/CLOSE logs correctly (recent commit suggests partial work here)
Establish a daily paper trading log review process to catch silent failures
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Test suite passes at >90%. A 5-day paper trading run produces verifiable trade logs with entry/exit prices, P&L, and agent reasoning recorded correctly. No silent failures observed in logs.

product_roadmap.goal: Build the measurement infrastructure to know whether signals are predictive, then apply research-backed improvements to signal quality and entry discipline.
product_roadmap.rationale: The research findings are unanimous: IC-weighted dynamic scoring substantially outperforms static equal-weight models. But IC weighting requires rolling IC measurement to exist first. You cannot improve what you cannot measure. This phase installs the measurement layer, then applies the highest-confidence signal improvements that research supports.
features: Signal Decay Monitor & Freshness Dashboard — rolling IC per dimension, weekly cadence
Alpha Decay Chart — per-trade forward return distribution tracker
Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite — cross-sectional standardisation before compositing
Multi-Timeframe Signal Confirmation — daily/4H/1H alignment gate before entry
Regime-Gated Momentum / Mean-Reversion Signal Router — VIX-proxy two-state regime gate
Unusual Options Activity (UOA) Signal Dimension — research-backed, free via yfinance
Options Flow Proxy Signal via Put/Call Ratio — free yfinance options chain
Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop — typed Signal objects, separates generation from execution (prerequisite for IC tracking and WebSocket broadcast)
IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scorer — replace equal-weight composite with rolling IC weights (after measurement infrastructure is live)
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Rolling IC dashboard shows per-dimension predictive power scores updating weekly. Signal composite uses IC-weighted scoring. Multi-timeframe gate is active. Paper trading win rate and Sharpe ratio are being tracked with at least 30 trades in the log.

product_roadmap.phase_name: Phase 2 — Risk Infrastructure & Position Sizing Robustness
product_roadmap.goal: Harden the risk layer so position sizing is volatility-aware, drawdown-protected, and correlation-conscious before any live capital is deployed.
product_roadmap.rationale: Research is clear that plain Kelly is dangerous for live trading. Before transitioning to live trading, the system needs fractional/adaptive Kelly, ATR volatility targeting, a drawdown circuit breaker, and correlation awareness. These are capital-safety features, not performance features — they must precede live trading.
features: ATR Volatility-Targeting Position Size Layer — σ*/σ_t formula
VIX-Rank Adaptive Fractional Kelly Sizer — dynamic Kelly fraction scaled to VIX regime
Drawdown Brake — three-tier equity curve circuit breaker
Dual-Tranche Exit Strategy — split positions into two tranches on entry
Intra-Portfolio Rolling Correlation Guard with Sector Cap — 30-day pairwise correlation flag
Portfolio Heat Monitor with Correlation-Adjusted Exposure Cap — pre-order check
Monte Carlo Stress Testing — 1000x shuffled backtest sequences for drawdown distribution
Walk-Forward Backtesting — rolling 6mo train / 1mo test validation
Slippage & Execution Cost Model for Backtester — realistic per-trade slippage
Anchored VWAP Entry Gate — weekly AVWAP binary gate for entries
Time-of-Day Seasonality Filter — avoid low-quality execution windows
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Backtester produces walk-forward validated Sharpe >1.0 and max drawdown <15% across 6+ months of paper data. Monte Carlo stress test passes. Drawdown brake is tested and triggers correctly in simulation. Position sizing adapts to volatility regime visibly in logs.

product_roadmap.goal: Transition one account from paper to live trading with hard capital limits, full monitoring, and kill-switch capability.
product_roadmap.rationale: Only after stable paper trading with validated signals and hardened risk management should live capital be risked. This phase adds the operational safeguards — real-time alerting, manual override, and the per-account config system — needed for a single live account.
features: Per-Account Config Profiles — account-aware profile system replacing flat CONFIG
Per-Account Risk Profile Config Schema with Multi-Tier Presets — RiskProfile dataclass
Parameterise account_id Through Orders and Risk — audit and replace all CONFIG['active_account'] reads
AccountDecisionAgent Class — per-account downstream agent from 6-agent pipeline
Per-Account Local Dashboard — positions, P&L, trade history per account on port 8080
Telegram Bot — Notifications and Quick Commands — trade alerts with AI rationale
Regime-Conditional Signal Weighting (Momentum vs Mean-Reversion Gate) — live regime adaptation
Alpha Decay Monitor — Per-Signal IC Rolling Health Check — alert on signal degradation
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: One live IBKR account is trading. Telegram alerts fire on every trade. Dashboard shows live P&L. Account has operated profitably or within acceptable drawdown for 30 consecutive trading days. Kill-switch (drawdown brake) has been manually tested.

product_roadmap.goal: Move the bot from local machine to a cloud VPS so it runs 24/7 without the founder's laptop being on.
product_roadmap.rationale: Reliability and uptime are prerequisites for trusting the system with live capital across market hours. Cloud hosting also establishes the container infrastructure needed for multi-user expansion.
features: Cloud VPS Setup and Deployment — Ubuntu 22, Docker, shared brain container
Connection Manager — Multi-Account IBKR — multiple ib_insync instances with unique ports
Reconnection Logic — Backoff, Sequence, State Sync — exponential backoff, state sync on reconnect
WebSocket Signal Broadcaster — brain publishes signals to containers in real time
Per-Account Local Dashboard — cloud-accessible version
Rolling CVaR Dashboard Metric and Per-Account Risk Budget — operational risk visibility
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Bot runs uninterrupted on cloud VPS for 14 consecutive days. Reconnection logic survives a simulated network drop. WebSocket broadcast is confirmed working. No manual intervention required for normal operation.

product_roadmap.goal: Onboard the first 3-5 friends/family users with isolated accounts, personal dashboards, and guided setup.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the first time multiple users share the infrastructure. Isolation, authentication, and onboarding UX must work before inviting users who are not the founder.
features: Per-User Docker Compose Containers — isolated containers per user with own account agents and IB gateway
Signal Registry — Per-User Favourite Symbols — brain tracks which symbols each container has requested
Per-User Dashboard Authentication — login, sessions, role isolation
One-Time User Setup Screen — guided IBKR credentials + risk profile setup on first login
Web Dashboard — Live Positions and P&L — browser-based, per-user
Per-Account Risk Limits — independent max drawdown, position size, sector concentration per user
Account-Aware Order Router — route orders to correct IBKR account by strategy assignment
Cross-Account Correlation Guard — flag unintended correlated exposure across accounts
Unified Trade Journal — cross-account logging with account tags
Account Performance Comparison — side-by-side Sharpe, max drawdown dashboard
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: 3+ friends/family users are live. Each user can log in, see only their own account, configure risk settings, and receive trade notifications. Founder can see aggregate view. No cross-account data leakage observed in 30-day test period.

product_roadmap.goal: Harden the platform for reliability, performance, and scalability beyond the beta cohort.
product_roadmap.rationale: Beta will surface operational issues that need resolving before the platform is considered production-grade. This phase addresses advanced ML features, monitoring, and platform resilience.
features: Meta-Labeling Secondary Classifier — binary ML meta-model on top of primary signal pipeline
Triple Barrier Method for ML Training Labels — replace binary up/down labels
HMM Regime Detection — 3-state HMM on vol + RSI for regime classification
Regime-Conditioned Position Size Scalar — connect HMM to position sizing
Walk-Forward Weight Calibration — walk-forward optimization of signal weights
Signal Validation with Alphalens — IC scores, turnover, decay curves per dimension
Signal Correlation De-Duplication Guard — remove redundant correlated signal dimensions
Dynamic Volatility-Based Position Sizing — ATR-based volatility ceiling on Kelly output
Order Flow Imbalance Detection — institutional accumulation approximation
News Sentiment Regression Scorer (Yahoo RSS → FinBERT) — 10th signal dimension
Claude Sentiment Scoring Pipeline — structured sentiment as signal dimension
SEC Form 4 Insider Cluster Buying Dimension — EDGAR RSS insider signal
Reddit Mention Velocity Dimension — quantitative WSB/stocks mention velocity
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Platform handles 10+ concurrent users without degradation. ML meta-model is trained on live trade history. All signal dimensions have validated IC scores. System uptime >99% over 60 days.

immediate_next_action.action: Fix the test suite to achieve a green baseline — resolve agent JSON parse errors, data_collector mock failures, and orders.py IBKR stub issues so the paper trading loop can be trusted.
immediate_next_action.rationale: The test suite is at 0% pass rate. Every other improvement — signal quality, risk hardening, live trading — depends on knowing the system works. The recent commits show active stabilisation work (reqPnL stub, logging, retry backoff) but tests are still failing. Until the foundation is verified, all feature work is being built on unvalidated ground. This is the highest-leverage single action available.
immediate_next_action.cowork_prompt: We need to get the Decifer test suite to a green baseline. The current pass rate is 0% with errors in three areas: (1) agents.py:442 — Final agent JSON parse error when responses are not valid JSON, (2) data_collector.py — network/timeout errors causing test failures that should be handled gracefully in test mode, (3) orders.py — IBKR connection stub issues with openTrades(), reqPnL, and reqGlobalCancel. Please: (a) review the current test failures across all 32 test files, (b) fix the agent JSON parse error handling so malformed LLM responses are caught and defaulted gracefully rather than crashing, (c) ensure data_collector tests use proper mocks so they pass without network access, (d) fix orders.py IBKR stub issues so tests can run without a live TWS connection. Target: >90% test pass rate. Do not add new features — only fix what is broken.

untracked_ideas.title: Paper Trading Performance Dashboard — Automated Daily P&L and Win Rate Report
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision (paper trading stage must prove profitability before live) and current stage assessment
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no feature in the pipeline for a structured paper trading scorecard — a daily or weekly automated report showing cumulative P&L, win rate, average win/loss, Sharpe, and drawdown from paper trades. Without this, there is no objective exit criterion for the paper trading stage and no evidence to justify moving to live trading.

untracked_ideas.title: Stage Gate Approval Process — Formal Criteria to Advance Between Stages
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by the staged vision (paper → live → cloud → multi-user) and absence of any defined exit metrics in current feature specs
untracked_ideas.rationale: The vision defines 5 stages but no feature or document defines what metrics must be achieved to graduate from one stage to the next. Without formal exit criteria (e.g. 'paper trading must show Sharpe > X over N trades before live trading is enabled'), advancement decisions become arbitrary and risk is introduced prematurely.

untracked_ideas.title: Broker Connection Health Monitor with Auto-Restart
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by orders.py IBKR connection errors in test logs and cloud hosting requirements in Phase 4
untracked_ideas.rationale: Multiple error logs show IBKR connection drops causing silent order failures. For a cloud-hosted system running unattended, there needs to be a health monitor that detects dead IBKR gateway connections and attempts auto-restart before orders are missed. This is distinct from the reconnection logic feature (which handles re-subscribing after reconnect) — this is about detecting the dead connection in the first place.

untracked_ideas.title: Trade Rationale Audit Log — Human-Readable Record of Why Each Trade Was Taken
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by multi-user vision (users need to understand why trades happened in their account) and agent architecture
untracked_ideas.rationale: When friends and family are using the platform with real money, they will ask 'why did the bot buy this?' There is no feature spec for a persistent, human-readable rationale log that captures the agent consensus reasoning, signal scores, and regime state at the time each trade was placed. This is both a trust-building feature for users and a debugging tool for the founder.

untracked_ideas.title: Maximum Concurrent Positions Cap
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by risk management research findings (fractional Kelly, portfolio heat) and multi-account architecture
untracked_ideas.rationale: The risk features address position sizing and correlation but there is no explicit feature for a hard cap on total number of concurrent open positions. Without this, in a strong trending market the bot could open many positions simultaneously, concentrating capital in ways the volatility-targeting formula alone does not prevent.
untracked_ideas.suggested_phase: Phase 2 — Risk Infrastructure & Position Sizing Robustness

untracked_ideas.title: Graceful Market Close / Pre-Weekend Position Management
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by paper trading operational requirements and gap risk for live trading
untracked_ideas.rationale: There is no feature spec for handling end-of-day or pre-weekend position management — whether to flatten positions before close, reduce size ahead of weekends/earnings, or hold overnight. Gap risk is a significant source of drawdown for momentum strategies. This should be a configurable per-account risk setting.
untracked_ideas.suggested_phase: Phase 2 — Risk Infrastructure & Position Sizing Robustness

risks.risk: 0% test pass rate means the paper trading system cannot be verified as working correctly — trades, risk checks, and agent consensus may be silently misbehaving without detection
risks.mitigation: Immediate priority: fix all failing tests before any new feature work begins. Establish a rule that no feature is merged unless tests remain green.

risks.risk: 50 active features spanning multiple future stages are being designed and partially built simultaneously, creating integration debt and sequencing confusion — multi-user cloud features are in-flight while single-account paper trading is unvalidated
risks.mitigation: Freeze feature additions beyond Phase 0 and Phase 1 items. Reassign all active multi-user and cloud features (MA1, MA2, MA3 tags) to backlog with explicit stage gate dependencies. Use the roadmap phases as a strict WIP limit.

risks.risk: No measurable exit criteria exist for the paper trading stage — without defined performance thresholds (Sharpe, win rate, drawdown), the decision to go live will be arbitrary and potentially premature
risks.mitigation: Define explicit paper trading graduation criteria before Phase 1 begins: minimum trade count, minimum Sharpe ratio, maximum drawdown, minimum consecutive days without critical errors.

risks.risk: The IC-weighted signal scoring (the highest-confidence research-backed improvement) is being built before the IC measurement infrastructure exists — there is no rolling IC tracker yet, so the weights would be arbitrary
risks.mitigation: Enforce the correct build sequence in Phase 1: Signal Decay Monitor and Alpha Decay Chart must ship and collect data before IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scorer is implemented.

risks.risk: bot.py at 2787 lines is a monolith — the Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop feature is active but not yet shipped, meaning signals and execution are still coupled, making testing and staged rollout difficult
risks.mitigation: Prioritise Extract Signal Objects as an early Phase 1 item — it is a prerequisite for IC tracking, WebSocket broadcast, and clean testing of the signal pipeline independently of order execution.

risks.risk: IBKR connection drop errors in orders.py are logged but the system's behaviour when the connection is lost mid-order is not clearly defined or tested — live trading with silent connection failures could result in untracked open positions
risks.mitigation: Add explicit connection-loss handling tests covering the scenario where an order is submitted but the confirmation is never received. Define and implement a safe default behaviour (assume order is open, do not resubmit without explicit check).

risks.risk: Friends and family will connect real broker accounts — any bug in multi-user isolation (wrong account receiving orders, credentials leaking between containers) could cause financial harm and destroy trust
risks.mitigation: Multi-user features must include mandatory cross-account isolation testing before any beta user is onboarded. The Cross-Account Correlation Guard and Per-User Docker isolation must be tested with synthetic accounts before real accounts are connected.

risks.risk: Two duplicate entries exist in active and backlog for several features (IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scoring appears twice in active; HMM Regime Detection appears in both active and backlog; several MA-tagged features duplicate B-tagged features) — this creates confusion about what is actually being built
risks.mitigation: Audit the feature pipeline and deduplicate. For each duplicate pair, canonicalise one entry with the fuller spec and archive the other. Assign clear ownership to prevent parallel implementation of the same feature.

roadmap_gaps: Several capabilities implied by the vision are absent from the feature pipeline entirely: (1) A paper trading performance scorecard with automated daily/weekly metrics (Sharpe, win rate, drawdown) — required to objectively decide when to go live, but nothing in the pipeline tracks this. (2) Formal stage gate criteria — the vision defines 5 stages but no feature or document defines the measurable thresholds required to advance between them. (3) A trade rationale audit log accessible to non-technical users — when family members ask 'why did the bot buy NVDA with my money?', there is no feature to answer this. (4) Graceful end-of-day and pre-weekend position management — gap risk handling for overnight and weekend holds is unaddressed in any spec. (5) A maximum concurrent positions hard cap — correlation guards and heat monitors exist but no simple count-based position limit. (6) Broker connection health monitoring with auto-restart — distinct from reconnection logic, this is about detecting a dead gateway connection before an order is missed. (7) User-facing risk explanation UI — users selecting a risk profile need plain-language explanation of what 'aggressive' vs 'conservative' means in dollar terms for their account size; no UX spec exists for this. (8) Incident response playbook — what happens when the live bot takes an unexpected large loss, a position cannot be closed, or an IBKR API change breaks the system; no operational runbook or escalation procedure is defined anywhere in the pipeline.
